description_wiki: Elko County is a county in the northeastern corner of Nevada, United States.

description: city in and county seat of Washoe County, Nevada, United States
description_wiki: Reno (, REE-noh) is a city in the northwest section of the U.S. state of Nevada, along the Nevada–California border.

description: division of the Nevada Department of Conservation and Natural Resources
United States federal government public work relief program part of the New Deal
description_wiki: The Civilian Conservation Corps (CCC) was a government work relief program that ran from 1933 to 1942 in the United States for unemployed, unmarried men ages 18–25 and eventually expanded to ages 17–28, who volunteered amid widespread unemployment.

description: Protected area surrounding a key fort in the development of Nevada
description_wiki: Fort Churchill State Historic Park is a state park of Nevada, United States, preserving the remains of a United States Army fort and a way station on the Pony Express and Central Overland Routes dating back to the 1860s.
The Great Depression was a severe global economic downturn from 1929 to 1939.

description: president of the United States from 1933 to 1945 (1882–1945)
description_wiki: Franklin Delano Roosevelt (January 30, 1882 – April 12, 1945), also known as FDR, was the 32nd president of the United States, serving from 1933 until his death in 1945.

description: defunct American railroad (1903–1983) known for its Feather River route.
description_wiki: The Western Pacific Railroad (reporting mark WP) was a Class I railroad in the United States.

aliases: The Entertainment Capital of the World
The Gambling Capital of the World
description: seat of Clark County, and largest city in state of Nevada, United States
description_wiki: Las Vegas, colloquially referred to as Vegas, is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Nevada and the seat of Clark County.

description: city in and county seat of White Pine County, Nevada, United States
description_wiki: Ely (, EE-lee) is the only city in and the county seat of White Pine County, Nevada, United States.
